meta.title: Dropped Objects Prevention — DROPS Survey & Register
meta.subtitle: Named installation, dropped-object survey extract
meta.author: HSE Marine & Offshore
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This extract presents the dropped-objects-prevention basis for the named installation: the DROPS survey scope, the dropped-object register (static vs dynamic taxonomy), the reliable-securing controls and the exclusion zones. The control posture is reliable securing first; consequence bands trace to the public m·g·h method with user-supplied mass and fall height (licensed DROPS Calculator threshold values are recorded as the user's band, not recomputed).

Lead control: every at-height item is reliably secured (primary fixing + secondary retention) and surveyed, with exclusion zones below at-height work — 'hard hats for the people below' is the residual, never the headline.

items: Named installation / vessel and area surveyed
At-height structures: derrick/mast, crane boom & pedestal, monkeyboard & fingerboard, flare tip & boom, riser/wellhead deck, piping & small-bore fittings, lighting & instruments
Fixed and potential dropped objects identified per DROPS Recommended Practice (2017)

sections.caption: Dropped-object register — taxonomy, public m·g·h impact energy (user-supplied mass/height), user's DROPS band ([GAP] where unconfirmed; licensed thresholds never reproduced), securing status

rows.title: At-height item with no recorded securing standard
rows.description: An at-height item carries no recorded primary fixing / secondary retention standard — flagged as an immediate high-priority finding pending the reliable-securing standard.

rows.title: Reliable securing verified on surveyed items
rows.description: Surveyed at-height items have a recorded primary fixing + secondary retention standard and a current DROPS inspection.

rows.control: Remove non-essential items from height; design out the dropped-object source
Primary fixing + secondary retention (tethering/lanyards) to a reliable-securing standard
DROPS survey + register, inspection regime, exclusion/red zones below at-height work

rows.action: Apply the reliable-securing standard to the unsecured at-height item and record it in the register
Confirm the licensed DROPS Calculator consequence bands with the SME and close the [GAP] band values

sections.text: Consequence bands trace to user-supplied mass and fall height via the public m·g·h method; the licensed DROPS Calculator threshold values stay [GAP] / user-confirmed and are never reproduced.

items: DROPS Recommended Practice (2017) + DROPS Reliable Securing — survey, taxonomy, reliable-securing hierarchy, exclusion zones
IADC HSE Guidelines Section 16 (Dropped Objects); API RP 2D (offshore crane O&M) / API RP 54 (drilling & servicing)
